mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals near-term household demand strength, raising expected corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 411591 as of one month before 2012-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests, or if the 3-year rolling Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, causing equity investors to mark down earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 7.9 as of one month before 2012-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero and consumer inflation is not accelerating, policy remains liquidity-supportive and discount rates stay compressed, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is below its prior 12-month pace
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 0.16 as of one month before 2012-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate and non-accelerating-inflation condition does not deliver above-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns with t-test p <= 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A strong uptrend in housing starts signals residential investment recovery and improving household balance-sheet confidence, supporting cyclical earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 15% versus 6 months earlier
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 872 as of two months before 2012-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST recoveries have median 60-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional median or a 3-year rolling Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production combined with weakening payroll growth signals that real activity is losing momentum, reducing expected margins and revenues and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls over 3 months and PAYEMS 3-month growth is below its prior 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 97.0328 as of two months before 2012-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO and PAYEMS slowdown condition is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer prices while the federal funds rate remains near zero raises fears of future policy tightening and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 4% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 231.751 as of one month before 2012-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation accelerations at the zero lower bound have nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or hit-rate against the predicted negative sign below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains alongside a stable or falling unemployment rate signal broad employment improvement, strengthening consumption expectations and risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises for 3 consecutive months and UNRATE does not increase over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 133755 as of one month before 2012-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-growth and stable-unemployment condition fails to produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than unconditional returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in real GDP growth signals broad demand weakening and lower aggregate earnings momentum, causing investors to reduce equity exposure and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth falls for 2 consecutive available releases
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 13616.2 as of four months before 2012-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered GDPC1 decelerations are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if the average forward return is not below baseline.
